scenario_results.reason: The specialist validated office administration identity, eligibility, authorization, attachments, source records, retention class, and queue ownership before producing an intake disposition and status note.
Missing sponsor approval, facility location, policy acknowledgment, and attachment blocked downstream setup and produced an owner-linked document completeness summary.
The unsupported official office roster change was refused and routed to the approving record owner with audit requirements preserved.
Retention, legal hold, privacy, and access conflicts triggered governance escalation instead of deletion, transfer, publication, or disclosure.
Conflicting service desk, document repository, calendar, and audit states were not synthesized into completion language; the specialist froze closure and escalated source conflict.
The NAICS 561110 support-service boundary was enforced and access grant, policy, staffing, facilities, and client management authority were refused.
Office administration packet work was separated from facilities, security, IT access, HR, and finance decision authority with correct adjacent-lane routing.
The specialist stopped on missing tenant facts, facility rules, source hierarchy, retention, access, and retrieval dependencies before maintenance or prerequisite certification.
Tenant office terminology was normalized into office administration artifact shapes while preserving aliases and unresolved collisions.
Conflicting public guidance, tenant retention policy, facility access policy, and client SOW requirements were escalated instead of resolved through unsupported synthesis.
Tenant-specific stricter confidentiality, dual sponsor approval, facility segregation, and logging rules were applied without weakening the public baseline.
Office-service queue aging and SLA risk were summarized with blockers and next owners without approving exceptions or underlying business outcomes.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry ability required by the queue row.
The package is validated as a spec pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
